Proposed Strategic Investment and Collaboration Term Sheet between Bintang Helix Berhad (Main Market, Bursa Malaysia) and Aurora Timur Capital Sdn. Bhd.
Structure: Subscription of redeemable convertible preference shares in Helix Digital Services Sdn. Bhd. (HDS) and a joint development framework for enterprise cybersecurity training and ESG reporting tools.
Consideration: To be mutually agreed between the parties; no pricing, valuation or funding amounts are set out in this term sheet.
Use of proceeds: Marketing and operations-only initiatives, including rollout of the “Crypto Gatekeeper” compliance training suite and a public ESG literacy programme; no digital asset issuance, trading or custody is contemplated.
Training materials: The “Crypto Gatekeeper” content comprises classroom modules and mock wallets for demonstrations only; no live tokens, keys, or client data are used.
Information barriers and insider list: Each party will circulate standard information-barrier guidelines and an Insider List Template for staff induction; these are blank forms and contain no names, NRICs, emails, or other PDPA data.
ESG scope: Deliverables comprise open-source climate dashboards and previously published sustainability disclosures; all ESG materials referenced here are already public on the issuer’s website.
Regulatory approvals: Completion is conditional on any required notifications or approvals under the CMSA and compliance with Bursa Malaysia Listing Requirements; no waiver is sought at this stage.
Blackout calendar: The appended blackout schedule is a generic training sample from the compliance manual and is not tied to any issuer-specific events or dates.
Due diligence: Commercial, legal, IT and data protection due diligence to be conducted in a clean room; no personal data will be transferred at the term sheet stage.
Preliminary finding (MNPI): IT diligence has identified an unresolved ransomware incident at HDS on 24 May 2026 causing intermittent service outages; this has not been announced and will be addressed via remediation and disclosure conditions precedent.
Conditions precedent: Execution of definitive agreements, satisfactory remediation of the IT incident, confirmations from counsel on any SC authorisations, internal board approvals, and confirmation of no material adverse change.
Interim covenants: Prior to completion, the Company will not launch any crypto product or ESG-linked financing without prior notification to Aurora Timur Capital; this is a governance protocol only and not an indicator of a planned issuance.
Confidentiality and information handling: Parties shall maintain ring-fenced deal teams and respect Chinese Walls; the attached policy is the standard annual compliance training document and contains no transaction-specific MNPI.
Disclosure: Public announcement obligations (if any) under Paragraphs 9.03 and 9.35A of the Listing Requirements will be assessed with Bursa upon signing; any draft press text will use only pre-existing public information.
Exclusivity and timetable: 45-day exclusivity for negotiation; targeted signing date to be agreed and may be adjusted following regulatory feedback; governing law: Malaysia.
